title: Work & School
shortSummary: Talk about your job, your studies, and daily working life in French.
culturalNote: French working culture values a clear separation between work and private life, and the right to disconnect after hours is taken seriously. The standard working week and generous holidays are part of the rhythm. Education is highly structured, and the baccalauréat exam at the end of high school is a major milestone. Qualifications and titles carry real weight.

keyPhrases.english: What do you do for work?
keyPhrases.pronunciation: kuh fet VOO don lah VEE

keyPhrases.native: Je travaille de neuf heures à dix-sept heures
keyPhrases.english: I work from nine to five
Have a good rest of the day!

grammar.rule: Verbs of profession without an article
grammar.explanation: State your profession with être plus the job, no article: Je suis ingénieur (I'm an engineer). For where you work, use travailler chez (a company) or travailler dans (a sector).
grammar.commonMistake: Adding un: it is Je suis ingénieur, not Je suis un ingénieur.

grammar.explanation: étudier means to study a subject formally (j'étudie la médecine), while apprendre means to learn a skill or language (j'apprends le français). Both take a direct object.
grammar.commonMistake: Using étudier for a language you are picking up casually. Use apprendre for learning.

dialogue.setup: At a party, Nora and David get to know each other and talk about work.

lines.speaker: Nora
lines.native: Et toi, que fais-tu dans la vie, David ?
lines.english: And you, what do you do for work, David?

lines.speaker: David
lines.native: Je travaille comme programmeur dans une petite entreprise.
lines.english: I work as a programmer at a small company.

lines.speaker: Nora
lines.native: J'étudie encore. J'étudie la médecine à l'université.
lines.english: I'm still studying. I study medicine at university.

lines.speaker: Nora
lines.native: Oui, mais ça me plaît. Bonne fin de journée !
lines.english: Yes, but I enjoy it. Have a good rest of the day!

quiz.question: How do you ask 'What do you do for work?' (formal)
quiz.explanation: A common, natural way to ask about someone's job.

quiz.question: Je travaille _____ une banque. (in)
quiz.explanation: travailler dans is used for a sector or place.

quiz.question: Which sentence is correct?
quiz.explanation: French drops the article before professions.

quiz.question: Which verb means 'to learn (a language)'?
quiz.explanation: apprendre is to learn a skill or language.
